reasoning: The Lean signature assumes `[CommRing R] [IsDomain R] [GCDMonoid R]`. The extra `[GCDMonoid R]` is not a substantive additional hypothesis: the informal condition (i) already implies every integral domain satisfying it admits a GCDMonoid structure (define gcd via (i)), and since gcds in a domain are unique up to units, "the GCDMonoid gcd equals ra+sb" is equivalent to "some gcd equals ra+sb". Hypothesis h1 faithfully encodes condition (i) — `GCDMonoid.gcd a b` is a genuine gcd by the class axioms. Hypothesis h2 faithfully encodes condition (ii): the 0-indexed `ℕ → R` sequence vs. informal 1-indexed sequence and allowing N=0 vs. "positive integer N" are inessential encoding differences (if any N works, any larger N also works). The conclusion `IsPrincipalIdealRing R` combined with `IsDomain R` is Mathlib's standard encoding of "R is a PID".
